short_volatility.name: Duke Energy
short_volatility.bullish_case: DUK shows mean-reverting behavior with a mild AI-driven upside of about 1.1%. Flat term structure suggests theta capture; put skew provides tail hedges. Regulated cash flows and dividends support stability. A modest upside remains plausible if macro risk stays contained and catalysts align. Prior notes favored mean-reversion and flat term-structure; current data largely align.
short_volatility.bearish_case: Macro shocks or regulatory outages could push DUK lower. IV shows persistent downside skew and mean reversion may fail if SPY declines. A move to 118–122 within 4–8 weeks is plausible with negative catalysts; absent catalysts, downside risk remains elevated before reversion.

A disappointing quarter or rising costs could trigger further pressure toward $60 or lower by late 2026, especially if market-wide volatility spurs hedging demand.

short_volatility.name: Best Buy
short_volatility.bullish_case: Known : BBY ≈$78.52 and the BBY term structure is in backwardation (near-dated IV rich vs later). Inferred : volatility may normalize faster than price if the downside tail isn’t repriced. The return-surface likelihood clusters around modest positive 1–2yr outcomes, while the vol-surface is most extreme far OTM (less ATM “cliff” pricing). SPY IV looks lower/flatter → not obviously a broad risk-off
short_volatility.bearish_case: Known : Helium AI price point forecast is about −4.1% today. Inferred : BBY downside skew remains valuable; if consumer/margin narrative disappoints, mean reversion could overshoot downward. Vol-surface and persistent backwardation would keep downside gamma expensive. Your earlier downside-tail scenario didn’t fully pay yet, but it’s still uncertain around the next expiry cluster (Jun 18–Jul 17).

short_volatility.name: Gold Fields
short_volatility.bullish_case: GFI's term structure is flat with IV in the mid-50s across horizons, per Helium's GFI chart. Helium AI forecasts about +5.3% upside, supported by a mild gold bid and a steady SPY backdrop. With stable near-term IV and a modest call skew, a slow grind toward the low- to mid-40s before expiry is plausible, aided by theta decay and limited downside risk in the current vol regime.
short_volatility.bearish_case: A macro shock or gold weakness could push GFI toward 38–39 by expiry. Risk-off dynamics may lift near-term SPY IV and widen the GFI put-skew, eroding short-vol edge. Flat term structure offers little carry cushion, so a downside break paired with a vol spike could occur with 18–25% likelihood depending on macro/news flow.

short_volatility.name: Doximity
short_volatility.bullish_case: DOCS trades near 20 with a mean-reversion history. Buybacks and AI monetization could unlock growth; the return surface favors near-term reversion toward the mid-30s. Elevated IV supports premium collection. A positive catalyst (strong bookings, buyback acceleration) could re-rate the stock toward the low-to-mid 30s within weeks.
short_volatility.bearish_case: DOCS could remain pressured due to weak bookings, AI monetization delays; The flat term structure indicates limited time premium. The stock could drift into the high-teens to low-20s if earnings guidance disappoints and macro risk persists. Absent a catalyst, price could stay range-bound or drift lower.
